root_text.sn55.14:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sabbaduggatibhayaṁ samatikkanto hoti.
root_text.sn55.14:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.14:1.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.14:1.7: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.14:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sabbaduggatibhayaṁ samatikkanto hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.14:1.1: “Mendicants, a noble disciple who has four things has gone beyond all fear of being reborn in a bad place.
translation_text.sn55.14:1.3: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.14:1.7: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.14:1.8: A noble disciple who has these four things has gone beyond all fear of being reborn in a bad place.”
